meta.title: Easter (Crucified and Risen) — John 18–20:23
meta.verseText: Always be joyful because you belong to the Lord. I will say it again. Be joyful!
meta.theme: You can have joy because Jesus is alive

questions.text: What special holiday were Jesus and His friends celebrating in Jerusalem?
questions.hint: It's a time when God's people remembered how He rescued them long ago.
questions.explanation: Jesus and His disciples were in Jerusalem celebrating Passover, which remembered how God rescued His people from Egypt.

questions.text: Which disciple brought soldiers to arrest Jesus?
questions.hint: He was one of Jesus' twelve disciples.
questions.explanation: Judas, one of Jesus' disciples, arrived with a group of soldiers carrying torches and weapons to arrest Jesus.

questions.text: What did the soldiers carry when they came to arrest Jesus?
questions.hint: They came at night and needed light.
questions.explanation: The soldiers carried torches and weapons when they came to arrest Jesus in the garden.

questions.text: When Jesus said 'I am he,' what happened to the soldiers?
questions.hint: Jesus' words were very powerful.
questions.explanation: When Jesus identified Himself using God's name 'I am,' the power of that truth knocked the soldiers down.

questions.text: Who was the Roman governor that questioned Jesus?
questions.hint: He couldn't find any reason to punish Jesus.
questions.explanation: Pontius Pilate was the Roman governor who questioned Jesus and found no basis for any charge against Him.

questions.text: True or False: Pontius Pilate found Jesus guilty of doing something wrong.
options: True — Pilate said Jesus was a criminal
False — Pilate found no reason to punish Jesus
False — Pilate never even spoke to Jesus
questions.hint: Think about what Pilate said to the crowd.
questions.explanation: Pilate told the crowd, 'I find no basis for a charge against him.' Jesus had done nothing wrong.

questions.text: What did the crowd shout when Pilate asked what to do with Jesus?
questions.hint: The crowd wanted something very bad to happen to Jesus.
questions.explanation: The crowd shouted 'Take him away! Crucify him!' even though Jesus had never done anything wrong.

questions.text: What is the name of the hill where Jesus was crucified?
questions.hint: It has a spooky-sounding name.
questions.explanation: The soldiers led Jesus to a hill called 'the Skull' where He was nailed to a wooden cross.

questions.text: What were Jesus' last words on the cross?
questions.hint: Jesus knew He had completed everything He came to do.
questions.explanation: After Jesus drank, He said 'It is finished,' meaning He had completed everything God sent Him to do.

questions.text: What was placed in front of Jesus' tomb after He was buried?
questions.hint: It was very heavy and hard to move.
questions.explanation: A large stone was rolled in front of the entrance to Jesus' tomb.

questions.text: Who was the first person to visit Jesus' tomb on Sunday morning?
questions.hint: She wanted to bring spices to care for Jesus' body.
questions.explanation: Very early on Sunday morning, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb before the sun was even up.

questions.text: What did Mary find when she arrived at the tomb?
options: The stone rolled away and the tomb empty
questions.hint: Something surprising had happened to the entrance.
questions.explanation: When Mary arrived, she saw the stone was rolled away, the tomb was open, and Jesus' body was gone.

questions.text: Which two disciples ran to the tomb after Mary told them it was empty?
questions.hint: They were two of Jesus' closest friends.
questions.explanation: Mary ran to tell Peter and John, two of Jesus' closest friends, and they both ran to the tomb as fast as they could.

questions.text: What did Peter and John find inside the empty tomb?
options: Strips of cloth that had wrapped Jesus' body
questions.hint: Something was left behind from the burial.
questions.explanation: When they looked inside, they saw the strips of cloth that Jesus' body had been wrapped in, but Jesus wasn't there.

questions.text: True or False: Mary immediately recognized Jesus when He appeared to her.
options: True — Mary knew it was Jesus right away
True — Mary had been expecting Jesus
False — Mary thought He was the gardener at first
False — Mary never saw Jesus at all
questions.hint: At first, Mary was confused about who was talking to her.
questions.explanation: Mary didn't recognize Jesus at first. She thought He was the gardener until Jesus said her name, 'Mary.'

questions.text: How did Mary finally recognize that it was Jesus?
questions.hint: Jesus said just one word and she knew.
questions.explanation: When Jesus said her name, 'Mary,' she turned around and knew it was Him. Jesus is alive!

questions.text: What did Mary say to the disciples after seeing Jesus alive?
questions.hint: It was the happiest news in the world.
questions.explanation: Mary Magdalene went to the disciples with the news and said, 'I have seen the Lord!'

questions.text: What is the Bottom Line for today's lesson?
options: You can have joy because Jesus is alive
questions.hint: It's all about joy and Easter.
questions.explanation: The Bottom Line is 'You can have joy because Jesus is alive!' — the central message of Easter.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: 'Always be _____ because you belong to the Lord.' (Philippians 4:4)
questions.hint: This word means really, really happy.
questions.explanation: Philippians 4:4 says 'Always be joyful because you belong to the Lord. I will say it again. Be joyful!'

questions.text: Fill in the blank: 'I will say it again. Be _____!' (Philippians 4:4)
questions.hint: The verse repeats this word twice because it's so important.
questions.explanation: The verse emphasizes joy by repeating it: 'Always be joyful... I will say it again. Be joyful!'

questions.text: Why is Easter called the most joyful day ever?
options: Because we get candy and eggs
Because Jesus rose from the dead and is alive
Because it's a day off from school
questions.hint: Think about what makes Easter different from every other day.
questions.explanation: Easter is the most joyful day because Jesus defeated death and came back to life — that's the best news ever!

questions.text: Why did Jesus allow the soldiers to arrest Him even though He had the power to stop them?
options: God had a plan to save people through Him
He didn't know they were coming
questions.hint: God had a special plan that Jesus came to complete.
questions.explanation: Jesus knew everything that was going to happen. He allowed it because God had a plan to rescue and save people through Him.

questions.text: What does 'It is finished' mean when Jesus said it on the cross?
options: Jesus completed everything God sent Him to do
questions.hint: Jesus had a mission from God the Father.
questions.explanation: 'It is finished' means Jesus had completed everything He came to do — paying for sin so we could be close to God again.

questions.text: True or False: On Saturday, Jesus' friends understood that He would rise again.
options: True — Jesus had told them clearly
False — they were sad and didn't understand yet
False — nobody had ever told them about it
questions.hint: Saturday was a quiet, confusing day for Jesus' friends.
questions.explanation: Even though Jesus had told them He would rise again, His friends didn't understand it yet. Saturday was full of sadness and questions.

questions.text: Why did Jesus give His life on the cross?
options: Because the soldiers forced Him to
Because He wanted to pay for our sins so we could be close to God
Because He was not strong enough to escape
questions.hint: It was all part of God's rescue plan.
questions.explanation: Jesus gave His perfect life to pay for sin. Because of what He did, we can be close to God and be His friends forever.

questions.text: What does it mean that Jesus 'defeated death'?
options: He was so strong nobody could hurt Him
He came back to life and death could not hold Him
He ran away from the tomb
questions.hint: Think about what happened on Easter Sunday morning.
questions.explanation: Jesus defeated death by rising from the dead. Death could not keep Him in the tomb — He is alive forever!

questions.text: Fill in the blank: Because Jesus is alive, we can be God's _____ forever.
questions.hint: The lesson talks about a special relationship with God.
questions.explanation: The lesson teaches that when we follow Jesus, we become God's forever friends. Jesus makes that friendship possible.

questions.text: True or False: The joy we get from Easter is only for Easter Sunday.
options: True — joy is only for special holidays
True — Easter joy fades after the weekend
False — we can have joy every single day because Jesus is alive
False — joy is only for grown-ups
questions.hint: Think about how long Jesus stays alive.
questions.explanation: Because Jesus is alive forever, we can have joy not just on Easter but every single day!

questions.text: Why was the name 'I am' so powerful when Jesus said it to the soldiers?
options: It was the same name God used with Moses at the burning bush
It scared the soldiers because it was loud
questions.hint: Think about the Old Testament and a famous bush.
questions.explanation: 'I am' is the name God used when He introduced Himself to Moses at the burning bush. By using it, Jesus was showing that He is God.

questions.text: Even while Jesus was hurting on the cross, what did He do for His mother?
options: He cared for her by asking a disciple to look after her
He told her not to cry
questions.hint: Jesus always cared about the people He loved.
questions.explanation: Even while suffering on the cross, Jesus told the disciple He loved to take care of His mother Mary, showing His love and care.

questions.text: How can you show joy this week because Jesus is alive?
options: By being grumpy and keeping to yourself
By worshiping, singing to God, and telling Him you love Him
By ignoring your friends and family
questions.hint: The lesson says one of the ways we show joy is through worship.
questions.explanation: One of the ways we show our joy is by worshiping, singing to God, and telling Him how much we love Him — at church and every day!

questions.text: When you feel sad or scared, what can you remember from today's lesson?
options: That you should hide your feelings
That Jesus is alive and His joy goes with you always
That you should just try harder
questions.hint: Jesus' joy doesn't go away when hard times come.
questions.explanation: Even when we feel sad or scared, we can remember that Jesus is alive, He loves us, and His joy goes with us through everything.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: Just like Mary told the disciples the good news, we can _____ others that Jesus is alive.
questions.hint: Mary ran to share the happiest news ever.
questions.explanation: Just like Mary ran to tell the disciples 'I have seen the Lord!' we can share the good news about Jesus with people around us.

questions.text: What should you do when you're at school or playing with friends and want to remember today's lesson?
options: Forget about it until next Sunday
Choose joy because Jesus is alive and loves you
Only think about games and fun
questions.hint: The memory verse says to 'always' be joyful.
questions.explanation: Philippians 4:4 reminds us to always be joyful because we belong to the Lord — at school, at home, and with friends.

questions.text: How is the joy from knowing Jesus different from being happy about getting a present?
options: Joy from Jesus lasts forever and doesn't depend on what happens to us
questions.hint: Think about how long Easter joy lasts versus how long a toy lasts.
questions.explanation: Happiness from presents fades, but joy from Jesus lasts forever because it's based on the truth that He is alive and loves us — no matter what.

questions.text: True or False: You have to wait until you're a grown-up to be a forever friend of God.
options: True — only adults can follow Jesus
True — kids are too young to understand
False — anyone, including kids, can follow Jesus and be God's friend now
False — nobody can really be God's friend
questions.hint: God's love and friendship have no age limit.
questions.explanation: The lesson teaches that when you decide to follow Jesus, you can be close with God right now. There's no age requirement to be God's forever friend!

questions.text: What book of the Bible is most of today's Easter story from?
questions.hint: It's the fourth book about Jesus' life.
questions.explanation: Today's Easter story comes primarily from John chapters 18–20, with a small portion from Luke 23 for the Saturday events.

questions.text: Who was Annas?
options: The father-in-law of the high priest
questions.hint: Jesus was taken to him first after being arrested.
questions.explanation: After arresting Jesus, the soldiers tied Him up and took Him to Annas, the father-in-law of the high priest.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: Friday was a hard day. Saturday was a quiet day. But Sunday changed _____.
questions.hint: Sunday was the most amazing day ever.
questions.explanation: Sunday changed everything! Jesus rose from the dead, defeating sin and death, and giving us joy that lasts forever.

questions.text: Why couldn't Jesus' friends visit the tomb on Saturday?
options: It was the Sabbath, a day of rest
They forgot where the tomb was
questions.hint: God had given His people a rule about this day.
questions.explanation: Since Saturday was the Sabbath, Jesus' friends had to rest, just as God had told His people to do. They couldn't visit the tomb.

termPairs.definition: A special time when God's people remembered how He rescued them from Egypt
The disciple who brought soldiers to arrest Jesus
The Roman governor who questioned Jesus and found Him not guilty
The hill where Jesus was crucified
The first person to visit the empty tomb on Sunday morning
Jesus' last words on the cross, meaning He completed His mission
The memory verse about always being joyful because you belong to the Lord
The two disciples who ran to the empty tomb after hearing Mary's news
The name God used with Moses that Jesus used to show He is God
The day of rest when Jesus' friends could not visit the tomb
Put to death by being nailed to a wooden cross
The place where Jesus' body was laid after He died, sealed with a large stone

keyWords.hint: The holiday that celebrates Jesus rising from the dead
A deep happiness that lasts because Jesus is alive
What happened to Jesus on the cross on Friday
Jesus came back to life on Sunday morning
The special celebration Jesus and His friends were having in Jerusalem
The disciple who betrayed Jesus and brought soldiers to arrest Him
The Roman governor who questioned Jesus
The place where Jesus was buried, sealed with a big stone
The day of rest between Friday and Sunday
Mary's last name — she was the first to see the risen Jesus
The city where Jesus was arrested and crucified
The book of the Bible where today's memory verse is found
The word that means coming back to life after dying
Two of these dressed in white appeared to Mary at the empty tomb

story.summary: During Passover in Jerusalem, Jesus was arrested, tried, and crucified on Friday even though He had done nothing wrong. His friends were heartbroken. On Saturday they waited in sadness. But on Sunday morning, Mary Magdalene found the tomb empty — Jesus had risen from the dead! He appeared to Mary and she ran to tell the disciples the joyful news: Jesus is alive! Because Jesus defeated death, we can have joy that lasts forever.

scenes.title: The Arrest in the Garden
scenes.description: After celebrating Passover and praying in a quiet garden, Jesus was arrested. Judas brought soldiers with torches and weapons. When Jesus said 'I am he,' the soldiers fell down. But Jesus allowed them to arrest Him because God had a plan to save people through Him.

scenes.title: The Trial and the Cross
scenes.description: Jesus was taken to Annas and then to Pontius Pilate. Even though Pilate found no reason to punish Jesus, the crowd shouted for Him to be crucified. Jesus was nailed to a cross on a hill called the Skull. He cared for His mother even while suffering, and His last words were 'It is finished.'

scenes.title: The Quiet Saturday
scenes.description: After Jesus died, His friends wrapped His body and placed it in a tomb sealed with a large stone. Saturday was the Sabbath so they could not visit. They waited in sadness, not understanding that God was still working and that Sunday was coming.

scenes.title: Sunday — He Is Alive!
scenes.description: Early Sunday morning, Mary Magdalene found the stone rolled away and the tomb empty. Peter and John ran to see. Mary stayed crying until Jesus appeared to her. When He said her name, she recognized Him. She ran to the disciples shouting, 'I have seen the Lord!' Jesus is alive and we can have joy forever!

scenes.title: Joy That Lasts Forever
scenes.description: Because Jesus rose from the dead, we can be God's forever friends. This joy isn't just for Easter — it's for every single day. Philippians 4:4 reminds us to always be joyful because we belong to the Lord. We can share this good news with everyone around us!
